title: Packed vs unpacked dimension confusion
body:
Compilation of the following module results in an error: `Packed array assignment pattern expects 8 element(s) in this context.`
```SystemVerilog
module a(output byte b [0:0]);
  assign b = '{'b1};
endmodule: a
```

### Expected Behavior
SystemVerilog allows implicit extension and truncation when assigning values. Since byte is a packed 8-bit type, the assignment pattern `'{ 'b1 }` should be interpreted as `8'b00000001` and this assignment should be valid.

### Unexpected Behavior
However, a similar module compiles without errors:
```SystemVerilog
module a(output byte b [0:0]);
  assign b = '{'b1,'b1,'b1,'b1,'b1,'b1,'b1,'b1};
endmodule: a
```

It seems like `byte b [0:0]` is treated as an unpacked array of 8 elements, rather than an unpacked array of one element, where the base type is `byte` (which is itself an 8-bit packed type)

This issue occurs in `Icarus Verilog version 13.0 (devel) (s20221226-572-gb74511974)`

Found by a [verilog model](https://github.com/DepTyCheck/verilog-model) using [deptycheck](https://github.com/buzden/deptycheck)
